task_completion.description: Verify the OpenBCI GUI application is open and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify an active EEG session is running in the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Scrolling EEG waveforms or data streams are visible in the main interface (Time Series widget).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent locates and interacts with the bandpass filter control.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is hovering over or clicking the bandpass filter button/dropdown, which initially displays '5-50 Hz'.

task_completion.description: Verify the bandpass filter options menu is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown menu or selection panel is expanded, showing various frequency range options including '1-50 Hz'.

task_completion.description: Verify the bandpass filter is successfully changed to the target range.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bandpass filter indicator or button in the GUI now explicitly displays '1-50 Hz' (or '1.0-50 Hz'), confirming the new setting is applied.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted directly with the OpenBCI GUI to change the setting.
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions to change the filter occur within the OpenBCI GUI window, rather than using terminal commands, scripts, or editing configuration files directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply type the target filter into a text editor or terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The '1-50 Hz' text appears specifically within the OpenBCI GUI's filter control element, not in a notepad, browser, or terminal window.

integrity.description: Verify the application state remains consistent and genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI maintains its normal layout with active EEG data; it does not appear to be a static screenshot or a mocked interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target application is OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2. It features a configurable bandpass filter, typically accessible via a dropdown or button on the Time Series widget. The task requires changing this filter from its initial state of '5-50 Hz' to the target state of '1-50 Hz'. Look for the filter control element in the GUI to confirm the final value is explicitly set to '1-50 Hz'.
